question_text: A 70 kg astronaut stands on a planet where g = 12 N/kg. They then travel to an orbiting space station where g = 0 N/kg. Calculate the change in their weight and explain what causes this change.
correct_answer: Weight on planet = 840 N; weight on station = 0 N; change = 840 N decrease. This occurs because the space station is in free fall around the planet, so there is no gravitational field effect at that location in the falling reference frame.
explanation: On the planet: W = 70 × 12 = 840 N. On the space station: W = 70 × 0 = 0 N (free fall). The station orbits in free fall, creating an apparent weightlessness even though gravity still acts to keep it in orbit.
